tasks.title: Apply pre-emergent herbicide
tasks.description: Apply a pre-emergent herbicide when soil temperatures reach 50°F (typically mid-March in Arkansas). Target crabgrass and annual weeds before they germinate.
tasks.petSafetyNote: Keep pets off treated areas until dry, typically 24 hours.

tasks.title: First spring fertilization
tasks.description: Apply a slow-release nitrogen fertilizer (1 lb N per 1,000 sq ft) after the lawn fully greens up. Avoid fertilizing too early — bermudagrass should be actively growing.
tasks.petSafetyNote: Allow fertilizer to water in before pets use the lawn.

tasks.title: Begin regular mowing
tasks.description: Start mowing when bermudagrass reaches 2 inches. Set blade to 1–1.5 inches. Remove no more than one-third of the blade in a single mowing.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Maintain weekly mowing at 1–1.5 inches. Bermudagrass is entering active growth in late March/early April in Arkansas.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue weekly mowing at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Check irrigation system
tasks.description: Test all sprinkler heads and drip lines before summer heat. Repair any broken heads.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue weekly mowing.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Mow at 1–1.5 inches, removing clippings only if they mat.

tasks.title: Summer fertilization
tasks.description: Apply 1 lb N per 1,000 sq ft using a balanced fertilizer. Bermudagrass is in peak growth and will respond well.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue weekly mowing.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Mow at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Water deeply 2–3 times per week
tasks.description: Apply 1 inch of water per week in two or three deep sessions rather than daily shallow watering. Water early morning to reduce evaporation.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue weekly mowing.

tasks.title: Water 1 inch per week
tasks.description: Continue deep, infrequent watering. Bermudagrass is drought-tolerant but performs best with consistent moisture in heat.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Maintain 1–1.5 inch height.

tasks.title: Spot-treat weeds
tasks.description: Apply post-emergent herbicide to any broadleaf weeds. Avoid spraying in temperatures above 90°F to prevent turf damage.
tasks.petSafetyNote: Keep pets off treated lawn for 24 hours after application.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue mowing at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Water 1 inch per week
tasks.description: Continue consistent watering schedule.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Mow at 1–1.5 inches in peak summer heat.

tasks.title: Water deeply 2–3 times per week
tasks.description: Arkansas summers are hot and humid. Water 1 inch per week in deep sessions.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue weekly mowing.

tasks.title: Second summer fertilization
tasks.description: Apply 1 lb N per 1,000 sq ft to support continued growth through peak summer. Use a slow-release formula.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Mow bermudagrass at 1–1.5 inches. In mid-June Arkansas heat, mow in the early morning or evening.

tasks.title: Water 1 inch per week
tasks.description: Apply 1 inch of water per week in 2–3 deep sessions. Water before 9am to minimize evaporation and fungal risk.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue mowing at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Water deeply
tasks.description: Continue 1 inch per week deep watering schedule.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Maintain 1–1.5 inch height through July.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue mowing.

tasks.title: Water deeply
tasks.description: Continue 1 inch per week deep irrigation.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Mow at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue mowing through peak summer.

tasks.title: Water deeply
tasks.description: Maintain 1 inch per week watering through August heat.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue weekly mowing at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Late summer fertilization
tasks.description: Apply 1 lb N per 1,000 sq ft no later than 6 weeks before expected first frost. In Arkansas, last fertilization is typically early August.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue mowing.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Mow at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Aerate if soil is compacted
tasks.description: Late August is a good time to aerate bermudagrass in Arkansas — the grass recovers quickly during warm weather. Core aerate to 3 inches depth.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue mowing.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Bermudagrass growth slows in early September.

tasks.title: Fall pre-emergent application
tasks.description: Apply pre-emergent herbicide in early September when soil temperatures drop below 70°F to prevent winter annual weeds like annual bluegrass.
tasks.petSafetyNote: Keep pets off treated areas for 24 hours.

tasks.title: Mow weekly
tasks.description: Continue mowing at 1–1.5 inches.

tasks.title: Reduce mowing frequency
tasks.description: Begin mowing every 10–14 days as bermudagrass growth slows in mid-September.

tasks.title: Reduce watering
tasks.description: As temperatures cool, reduce watering to 0.5 inch per week or less. Monitor for wilting before watering.

tasks.title: Final mow before dormancy
tasks.description: Mow one last time at 1.5 inches as bermudagrass begins to slow significantly. Do not mow below 1 inch heading into winter.

tasks.title: Winterization check
tasks.description: Blow out or drain irrigation lines before first hard freeze in Arkansas (typically late October to November). Disconnect hoses.
